body:
### Problem

when attempting to build neovim's bundled deps with emscripten for WebAssembly,
build fail: LuaJIT does not support the `wasm32` architecture.

`cmake.deps/cmake/BuildLuaJit.cmake` has no condition for Emscripten.

Emscripten satisfies CMake's `UNIX` condition, so it enters the
`elseif(UNIX)` and attempts to build LuaJIT with `emcc`.

LuaJIT's `.deps/build/src/luajit/src/lj_arch.h` only defines 7 architectures: x86, x64, ARM,
ARM64, PPC, MIPS32, MIPS64.

### Error
```bash
221:lj_arch.h:69:2: error: "Architecture not supported (in this version), see: https://luajit.org/status.html#architectures"
224:lj_arch.h:479:2: error: "No target architecture defined"
228:Makefile:270: *** Unsupported target architecture.  Stop.
229:make[3]: *** [Makefile:127: src/luajit] Error 2
230:make[2]: *** [CMakeFiles/luajit.dir/build.make:106: build/src/luajit-stamp/luajit-install] Error 2
231:make[1]: *** [CMakeFiles/Makefile2:190: CMakeFiles/luajit.dir/all] Error 2
232:make: *** [Makefile:91: all] Error 2
```

### Steps to reproduce

```bash
source /path/to/emsdk/emsdk_env.sh
emcmake cmake -S cmake.deps -B .deps -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release
emmake make -C .deps
```

> note: i also applied pull #37911 (not yet merged) to isolate this as
> next blocker after libuv is fixed.

### Expected behavior

build should complete 

### Workaround

passing these flags makes build successfully:

```bash
emcmake cmake -S cmake.deps -B .deps \
  -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release \
  -DUSE_BUNDLED_LUAJIT=OFF \
  -DUSE_BUNDLED_LUA=ON
```

### Proposed solution

detect Emscripten in `cmake.deps/CMakeLists.txt` and disable LuaJIT, falling back to PUC Lua 5.1:

```cmake
if(DEFINED EMSCRIPTEN OR CMAKE_C_COMPILER MATCHES "emcc$")
  set(USE_BUNDLED_LUAJIT OFF CACHE BOOL "" FORCE)
  set(USE_BUNDLED_LUA    ON  CACHE BOOL "" FORCE)
endif()
```

or any other preferred approach for handling this?

### Nvim version (nvim -v)

master (61f166ec40)

### Vim (not Nvim) behaves the same?

n/a

### Operating system/version

Arch Linux, kernel 6.19.6-arch1-1

### Terminal name/version

n/a

### $TERM environment variable

n/a

### Installation

build from repo (master, commit 61f166ec40) using Emscripten 5.0.2

comment_data.body:
@justinmk This is not a proposal to make LuaJIT build for WASM (which is explicitly listed as a non-goal, see https://github.com/LuaJIT/LuaJIT/issues/1112, so that is very unlikely to ever happen) but simply to automate building with PUC Lua for that target. 

Personally, I don't see WASM as a tier 1 (or 2) target that we need to (nor arguably should, for expectation management) support out of the box (yet) -- especially since the workaround is so simple and already well-documented.

> Proposed solution
> detect Emscripten in `cmake.deps/CMakeLists.txt` and disable LuaJIT, falling back to PUC Lua 5.1:

Detecting it and at least showing an error with a hint about the right flags, is a good first step.

As a followup step, detecting it and auto-fixing it might be wanted, or not, depending on what we normally do in other similar cases.

> Personally, I don't see WASM as a tier 1 (or 2) target that we need to (nor arguably should, for expectation management) support out of the box (yet) -- especially since the workaround is so simple and already well-documented.

We do need to start building in breadcrumbs so that more people will get interested in this, and so we can start running it in CI.

I think the "Proposed solution" here is already complete and just as simple (if not simpler) than adding an error message to CMake.

The question is:
1. Do we want to add WASM as an official target to eventually get to tier 1? (It sounds like the answer is yes, but as every target -- especially one that is not used by any maintainer -- comes with a cost, it's worth a discussion.)
2. Is CMake the right approach for it, or do we want to handle this through `build.zig` instead? (No point in doing the work twice for something we add _now_.)

Maybe a tracking issue about official WASM/Emscripten would good so we can get an overview of what exactly needs to be done to add _and maintain_ this target? (I admit to being somewhat biased due to my experiences over at tree-sitter...)

@bfredl

> passing these flags makes build successfully:

There's also `-DPREFER_LUA`, maybe that also works for your usecase?  (See https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/master/BUILD.md#puc-lua.)

tracking issue is https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/35567 , will add to it as we discover more requirements.

> 2\. Is CMake the right approach for it, or do we want to handle this through `build.zig` instead?

yep see https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/38076#issuecomment-3967670637 , but it seems that CMake is the shortest path for now.
@kumarvivek1752 to unblock you , please send a PR with your changes, and also set `PREFER_LUA` as mentioned. It sounds like you need https://github.com/neovim/neovim/pull/37911 as well?

give me somtime i'm exploring `build.zig` as it mentioned 

>this is something we are working on build.zig support for. So before investing time here, please look into whether `build.zig` ?already works for wasm, and if not, what is needed there

https://github.com/neovim/neovim/pull/37911#issuecomment-4030881139

i tried to exploring `zig.buid` it not supported yet for wasm, so i currently
work on cmake, as discussed last time, we have to use `PUC Lua 5.1`,i gard cmake to use puc lua while compiling for wasm,

- disable USE_BUNDLED_LUAJIT and enable USE_BUNDLED_LUA in cmake.deps for WASM
- appended `DEPS_PREFIX` to `CMAKE_FIND_ROOT_PATH` so `find_library()` searches bundled deps under emscripten's `ONLY` mode.
-  force `PREFER_LUA=ON` when `EMSCRIPTEN` is detected

it is generaiting build files, but currently blocked on cross compiling facing issue `nlua0` compiled for wasm
but build system tries to load it as native lua c extension for code generation, which fails with invalid ELF header.

current changess:
<details>
<summary>current diff</summary>

```diff
diff --git a/CMakeLists.txt b/CMakeLists.txt
index bbb93a6659..5dfb92ac58 100644
--- a/CMakeLists.txt
+++ b/CMakeLists.txt
@@ -102,6 +102,15 @@ else()
   endif()
 endif()

+# When cross-compiling with Emscripten, CMAKE_FIND_ROOT_PATH_MODE_LIBRARY is
+# set to ONLY by Emscripten toolchain, which restricts find_library() and
+# find_package() to search exclusively inside Emscripten sysroot.
+# Appending DEPS_PREFIX to CMAKE_FIND_ROOT_PATH allows CMake to also find the
+# WASM-compiled bundled dependencies in .deps/usr/.
+if(EMSCRIPTEN)
+  list(APPEND CMAKE_FIND_ROOT_PATH "${DEPS_PREFIX}")
+endif()
+
 list(INSERT CMAKE_PREFIX_PATH 0 ${DEPS_PREFIX})

 if(APPLE)
diff --git a/cmake.deps/CMakeLists.txt b/cmake.deps/CMakeLists.txt
index 901370b2e5..05a23ffca5 100644
--- a/cmake.deps/CMakeLists.txt
+++ b/cmake.deps/CMakeLists.txt
@@ -37,6 +37,13 @@ option(USE_BUNDLED_TS_PARSERS "Use the bundled treesitter parsers." ${USE_BUNDLE
 option(USE_BUNDLED_UNIBILIUM "Use the bundled unibilium." ${USE_BUNDLED})
 option(USE_BUNDLED_UTF8PROC "Use the bundled utf8proc library." ${USE_BUNDLED})

+# Emscripten/WASM: LuaJIT cannot be compiled by emcc.
+# Force PUC Lua and disable LuaJIT for WASM builds.
+if(EMSCRIPTEN)
+  set(USE_BUNDLED_LUAJIT OFF CACHE BOOL "" FORCE)
+  set(USE_BUNDLED_LUA    ON  CACHE BOOL "" FORCE)
+endif()
+
 if(USE_BUNDLED AND MSVC)
   option(USE_BUNDLED_GETTEXT "Use the bundled version of gettext." ON)
   option(USE_BUNDLED_LIBICONV "Use the bundled version of libiconv." ON)
@@ -87,6 +94,13 @@ endif()

 set(DEPS_INCLUDE_FLAGS "-I\"${DEPS_INSTALL_DIR}/include\" -I\"${DEPS_INSTALL_DIR}/include/luajit-2.1\"")

+# For WASM builds, PUC Lua headers are used instead of LuaJIT headers.
+if(EMSCRIPTEN)
+  set(DEPS_INCLUDE_FLAGS "-I\"${DEPS_INSTALL_DIR}/include\" -I\"${DEPS_INSTALL_DIR}/include/lua\"")
+else()
+  set(DEPS_INCLUDE_FLAGS "-I\"${DEPS_INSTALL_DIR}/include\" -I\"${DEPS_INSTALL_DIR}/include/luajit-2.1\"")
+endif()
+
 # If the macOS deployment target is not set manually (via $MACOSX_DEPLOYMENT_TARGET),
 # fall back to local system version. Needs to be done here and in top-level CMakeLists.txt.
 if(APPLE)
diff --git a/src/nvim/CMakeLists.txt b/src/nvim/CMakeLists.txt
index 15aa28c6ba..374fb2eea2 100644
--- a/src/nvim/CMakeLists.txt
+++ b/src/nvim/CMakeLists.txt
@@ -60,8 +60,26 @@ endif()

 # The unit test lib requires LuaJIT; it will be skipped if LuaJIT is missing.
 option(PREFER_LUA "Prefer Lua over LuaJIT in the nvim executable." OFF)
+
+# enable PREFER_LUA  for all WASM builds.
+if(EMSCRIPTEN)
+  set(PREFER_LUA ON CACHE BOOL "" FORCE)
+endif()
+
 if(PREFER_LUA)
-  find_package(Lua 5.1 EXACT REQUIRED)
+    if(EMSCRIPTEN)
+    # CMake's built-in FindLua.cmake fails under Emscripten due to
+    # CMAKE_LIBRARY_ARCHITECTURE suffix resolution in find_library().
+    # Directly reference the bundled PUC Lua from DEPS_PREFIX instead.
+    set(LUA_INCLUDE_DIR "${DEPS_PREFIX}/include")
+    set(LUA_LIBRARIES   "${DEPS_PREFIX}/lib/liblua.a")
+    if(NOT EXISTS "${LUA_LIBRARIES}")
+      message(FATAL_ERROR "Bundled liblua.a not found at ${LUA_LIBRARIES}. "
+                          "Run the deps build with emcmake first.")
+    endif()
+  else()
+   find_package(Lua 5.1 EXACT REQUIRED)
+  endif()
   target_include_directories(main_lib SYSTEM BEFORE INTERFACE ${LUA_INCLUDE_DIR})
   target_include_directories(nlua0 SYSTEM BEFORE PUBLIC ${LUA_INCLUDE_DIR})
   target_link_libraries(main_lib INTERFACE ${LUA_LIBRARIES})

```
</details>

i solved `nlua0` cross compilation by build it for linux first, then using that binary during the wasm build.
generating `nvim.wasm` , `nvim.js` using `-sERROR_ON_UNDEFINED_SYMBOLS=0` which allows linker to skip missing symbols instead hard failing.
so, it have many issues like: helptags tries to execute `nvim.js` but failed .etc

currently blocked libuv wasm incompatibility, like `uv__hrtime`,`uv__io_poll`,`uv__platform_loop_init/delete`,~25 more. which don't exist in wasm sandbox. i'm resarching around it.

any suggestions? and yes libuv is just an example others have issue too eg: `unibilium`.
there is a (supposedly) working impl already in https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/35567#issuecomment-3644771817 (wasm/wasi not emscripten, but that doesn't really matter?), did you look at it?

> i solved `nlua0` cross compilation by build it for linux first, then using that binary during the wasm build. generating `nvim.wasm` , `nvim.js` using `-sERROR_ON_UNDEFINED_SYMBOLS=0` which allows linker to skip missing symbols instead hard failing. so, it have many issues like: helptags tries to execute `nvim.js` but failed .etc
> 
The `nlua0` and the helptags issue you mentioned are fixed in #38076 & #38215. Have you tried these fixes? 

Regarding the missing libuv symbols, I explored adding stub implementations for the missing functions as a temporary workaround. However, Ig the proper long term fix would require adding a WASM implementation to libuv itself.

>  However, Ig the proper long term fix would require adding a WASM implementation to libuv itself.

That sounds like a hard blocker for the whole project, doesn't it? 

> there is a (supposedly) working impl already in https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/35567#issuecomment-3644771817

Looks like that project is doing exactly that: patching in shims for missing libuv implementations. I don't think that's reasonable to upstream, so an out-of-the-box WASM build for Neovim is currently not a possibility.

> I don't think that's reasonable to upstream, so an out-of-the-box WASM build for Neovim is currently not a possibility.

An exploratory PR will show what the damage is. Don't want to discourage that. Getting a CI job running for this, even if it's very hacky, gives a valuable baseline.

> That sounds like a hard blocker for the whole project, doesn't it?

It is actually a significant blocker, but not necessarily a hard one. As a short term workaround, stub implementations can be added for the missing symbols. It will act as patches that provide the missing functions. Would such stubs be acceptable to upstream into Neovim or would a proper WASM implementation in libuv be required first?

I see a bunch of patches in https://github.com/MuNeNICK/nvim-wasm/tree/master/patches/wasi-shim . That is a starting point. We can put stuff in e.g. `build.wasm/` or `deps.wasm/` directory.
(Note: preserve author credit to @MuNeNICK 's work.)

>The nlua0 and the helptags issue you mentioned are fixed in https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/38076 & https://github.com/neovim/neovim/pull/38215. Have you tried these fixes?

i  have  already your https://github.com/neovim/neovim/pull/38215. but it still helptags fails. 
i passed `-DNVIM_HOST_PRG` it resolved, my bad.

>I see a bunch of patches in https://github.com/MuNeNICK/nvim-wasm/tree/master/patches/wasi-shim . That is a starting point. We can put stuff in e.g. build.wasm/ or deps.wasm/ directory.

looking this.

edit:
>That sounds like a hard blocker for the whole project, doesn't it?

these are the libuv patch already done by him

https://github.com/MuNeNICK/nvim-wasm/blob/f5a8d3c251d280722c5ff7683ea730f99c0f6e39/scripts/patch/libuv_wasi_tail.lua

> i have already your [#38215](https://github.com/neovim/neovim/pull/38215). but it still helptags fails.
> 
Could you share the exact error you are getting with helptags? 
Are you passing `-DNVIM_HOST_PRG` pointing to a host native nvim binary when configuring?

progress so far, attempted to study libuv patch and applied to emcc, it partically solved issue, some func relvent only for `wasi-sdk`, need cleanup and use emcc specific func. 

current state: f~~acing runtime error (`E5009: $VIMRUNTIME is empty or unset`),also problem with (`shell failed to start: : sh`,`E79: Cannot expand wildcards`)~~ `ERR vim_mktempdir: tempdir root not owner` ,`wasmMemory` not able to read during  `fd_read` call path, causing tryRead loop hang .etc 

trying to further study how these things solved in `MuNeNICK/nvim-wasm`.

milestone.description: We don't plan to work on this, but will accept high quality contributions from someone who will own the feature and follow up on bug reports.
milestone.title: needs-owner
